message:
fix: init param shadowing method names in nested classes

Python allows __init__ parameters to have the same name as methods in
the class (e.g., `def __init__(self, real)` with `def real(self)`).
Zig doesn't allow this, so we rename conflicting params to `{name}_param`
and register them in var_renames so the body uses the renamed params.

Changes:
- Add wouldShadowMethodInClass() to detect param-method conflicts
- Add writeInitParamName() helper for param name generation
- Pass class_body to genInitMethodWithBuiltinBase/genInitMethodFromNew
- Track renamed params and clean up var_renames after body generation
